feat(span-buffer): Add killswitch for dropping spans (#92921)

Relay already protects the buffer, but if data is already in the topic
we need to have a solution as well. We could advance offsets manually
via script-runner, but this is probably better and more fine-grained.

where

ref(issues): Simplify overflow tooltips in header (#92941)

Related to [RTC-854: Issue details: add explanatory mouseovers to
titles](https://linear.app/getsentry/issue/RTC-854/issue-details-add-explanatory-mouseovers-to-titles)

build: Move lint config to `Cargo.toml`

This change also requires some changes to the `tests/` and `build.rs`, since the lints are now active for those as well.

feat(getting-started-docs): Add debug symbols upload instructions to unreal doc (#93004)

closes
https://linear.app/getsentry/issue/TET-534/new-project-onboarding-include-ability-to-create-an-auth-token-and-pre

perf(aci): Log slow evaluate_workflows_action_filters calls (#92908)

We think we're often slow because evaluate_workflows_action_filters is
taking multiple seconds.
This lets us confirm.

feat(nav): Tighten secondary nav animations slightly (#92876)

In the hopes of making this nav hover previews feel a bit snappier and
less heavy.

- Animation duration 80ms -> 60ms (don't think I can go lower than this
with 16ms per frame)
- Animation x offset 10px -> 4px
- Hover delay 200ms -> 150ms

ref(mobile-app): `&[u8]` instead of `&ByteView`

See https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cli/pull/2531/files#r2125991193

ref(sdk): Migrate to `get_isolation_scope`

Migrate calls of `sentry_sdk.Scope.get_isolation_scope` to `sentry_sdk.get_isolation_scope`. Calls where `Scope` is first imported and then where `Scope.get_isolation_scope` is called are left as-is; we will address those separately.

Split off from #92011.

feat(dashboards): Add platform icons on starred dashboards sidebar (#92947)

Take two of #92859. This time I added an error boundary around the
sidebar component with some telemetry I can alert on if something's
wrong with the API payload. I also added a null coalescing operator in
case it's still undefined, although the backend shouldn't be sending it
like that. This protects against all of the callsites where the issues
were raised about this component.

### Description

I found it confusing that after running `sentry-cli login --global`, the token in the `~/.sentryclirc` embeds the org slug, and then if I try to run something like `sentry-cli upload --org <org-id>`, it complains about a mismatch between the token and the id, even though they both represent the same thing.

Two ideas I have are:
1. reduce slug/id to a canonical version before comparing, so you never try to compare a slug to an id, even if they're for the same org
2. we need a way to know if we're dealing with a slug or id, since they're both just strings, and our IDs don't have any standard prefix. this indicates to me that we should actually have separate cli parameters like `--org-slug` and `--org-id` instead of simply `--org` that can accept either.

### Core or SDK?

Core Sentry product

### Which part? Which one?

Releases

### Description

The code example ```sentry-cli releases set-commits "$VERSION" --commit "my-repo@from..to"``` in the [cli release management](https://docs.sentry.io/cli/releases/#commit-integration) docs is missing a specification that ```from``` is excluded. I made the mistake to think it was inclusive.

### Suggested Solution

Would be great to specify that ```from``` is excluded.

Hey @armcknight – thanks for raising this. While I agree that in your specific case the warning is confusing, I believe the current UX is the best we can do.

I see the following problems with your suggested improvements:

1. Regarding the possibility of reducing the org slug or ID to a canonical version, this would require us to make an API request to the Sentry server to get the "canonical form" for the given ID or slug. I am unsure whether such an endpoint exists, and in any case, it seems unnecessary to make an extra API request just to determine whether a warning message should be output.
2.
   a. Adding separate parameters would require users to migrate existing usage of the `--org` parameter in any of their scripts to the new parameters.
   b. Since our API endpoints accept both org slugs and IDs as parameters, the two parameters would actually just be aliases of each other. The CLI is completely unaware of whether it is dealing with a slug or an ID, so we cannot reasonably validate whether the `--org-slug` is really a slug and not an ID, or whether an `--org-id` is an ID and not a slug. Having two parameters will just confuse users and add unnecessary friction.
   c. Hypothetically, if we did go with the option of the two separate parameters, it still would not solve the problem when there is an auth token and an `--org-id`. In this case, we would have no way of knowing whether the org ID matches the slug embedded in the auth token.

All this leads to my suggested solution: **users should never provide the `--org` parameter when they are using an org auth token**. We always ignore the manually-configured `--org` in favor of what is embedded in the token; the warning message you observed simply exists to inform the user of this.

### Next steps

I am closing this issue for the reasons provided above.

If you'd like, we can try to tweak the wording of the warning message to more clearly state that users should not provide the `--org` parameter when using an org auth token. 

We could also consider embedding the org ID in org auth tokens and checking the provided `--org` against both the org slug and the org ID. However, this improvement would only help with newly-generated org auth tokens, and would also require backend changes. It may be an excessive amount of work to just avoid emitting a warning; however, if there are other benefits to doing this it may be worth it (for instance, it would allow the org token to continue being used if the org slug is changed, since the ID should not change when editing the org slug if I understand correctly).

Thanks for clarifying! As there is an existing solution for your use case, I will close this issue for now.

Although adding a Python API is certainly a valid feature request, we would need to see more interest from users before we consider investing resources into creating and maintaining a Python API.
